column3: Ok, new project. gi, a coding agent based on go-ai and lessons learned from pi, piclaw and vibes. Create the project folder and Ask me what are my pain points so we can start on a spec
Created:

column1: To start the spec, tell me your pain points.
Use this if you want a structured dump:
1. **What frustrates you most** with current coding agents?
2. **Where pi/piclaw/vibes fall short** for your workflow
3. **What go-ai already solves** that you want to keep
4. **What you want gi to feel like**
- what gi should explicitly not do
- what would make gi clearly better than what you use now
If you want, I can also turn your answers into a first-pass spec with sections like:

column3: Ask me one question at a time
What frustrates you most about current coding agents?
Pi keeps changing upstream and I want a unified TUI/CLI/web ui
Got it: **upstream churn** and lack of a **single coherent TUI/CLI/web UI**.

column1: **What do pi/piclaw/vibes each get wrong for you today?**

column3: The turn handling mechanism is buggy and requires too much maintenance
Got it: **turn handling is fragile and high-maintenance**.

column1: **If gi had to fix just one thing about turn handling, what should it do differently?**

column3: It should be stupidly reliable and auto recover from provider failures, tool failures, context compaction, session sizes and rotation, etc. it should return control to the user solidly after each turn
Got it: **turn execution must be boringly reliable**, self-healing around provider/tool/context/session failures, and always **hand control back cleanly after every turn**.

column1: **When gi returns control after a turn, what exact state guarantees do you want?**

column3: What do you mean?
I mean: after any turn finishes, fails, or partially succeeds, **what must be true from the user’s point of view?**

column1: - no half-running hidden task is left behind
- retries are either done or clearly reported
- the agent is ready for the next prompt
- background tasks are detached and tracked
**After a turn ends, what guarantees should gi give you every time?**

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of those should be guaranteed**.

column1: **What kind of unified interface do you want first: TUI-first, CLI-first, or web-first?**

column3: All of them. Non negotiable. Some UX parity (except CLI, which should be used for batching and maintenance)
Got it: **TUI + web with UX parity**, and **CLI for batch/maintenance**. Non-negotiable.

column1: **Which UI should be the canonical one internally — the source of truth that the others map onto?**

column3: The web ui.
Got it: **web UI is the canonical interface**, and TUI/CLI should map onto it.

column1: **What are the must-have capabilities for gi v1?**

column3: In what category
I mean **core product capabilities** — the things gi must be able to do in v1, regardless of UI.

column1: **What are the must-have capabilities for gi v1?**

column3: Let’s do that in smaller chunks. Core features, core tools, etc
Good.

column1: **What are the core features gi must have in v1?**
Not tools yet — product behaviors. For example:

column3: No need for planning. No need for approvals either. Need scheduling. Skills.
Got it.

column1: **Do you want persistent memory in v1?**

column3: All the above. Modeled on piclaw’s system, with a more developed memory system coming later
Got it: **all of the above for v1**, modeled on **piclaw’s memory system**, with a more advanced memory layer later.

column1: **Do you want background/long-running tasks in v1, or should every interaction stay strictly turn-bounded except for scheduling?**

column3: I need a way to track background tasks yes
Got it: **background tasks are required in v1**, and they must be **trackable**.

column1: **What core tools must gi have in v1?**

column3: shell, read, write, edit, messages (message list search, extraction, attachments) schedule, exit, script. MCP and ACP in 1.1 for additional tools and subagents
Got it.
Use the joker Clojure interpreter to run scripts that can introspect gi and go-ai state and extend agent behavior
Got it: **`script` means running Joker/Clojure scripts** that can:

column1: **How much power should scripts have in v1?**
Or should some of that be restricted?

column3: Everything
Got it: **scripts get full power in v1**.

column1: **What should a skill be in gi?**
For v1, should a skill be:

column3: A prompt/SKILL.md with frontmatter and associated scripts
Got it: **a skill is `SKILL.md` + frontmatter + associated scripts**.

column1: **How should skills be discovered and loaded in v1?**

column3: Both. We will define the packaging approach later, but it should be simple to extract a specific skill from a GitHub URL and download it
Got it: **both local discovery and packaged skills**, and packaging should later support **simple extraction/download of a specific skill from a GitHub URL**.

column1: **How should the workspace be modeled in v1?**
- multiple project roots under one workspace

column3: One workspace, like piclaw
Got it: **one workspace root**, Piclaw-style.

column1: **Should gi be single-user only in v1, or do you want multi-user/multi-tenant support from the start?**

column3: I want three kinds of identities: users (the local user), agents and remote agents (1.1). I do need multi-session (and simultaneous contexts) from the start, sharing the same SQLite database to track state, chat, attachments, keychain, etc.
Got it.

column1: **How do you want sessions to behave in v1?**
- all of the above, or something else?

column3: I dont want them tied to a task. The rest is needed, but the branching strategy can be much simpler than pi.
Got it:

column1: - but with a **simpler branching model than pi**
**What should the simplified branching model be?**

column3: Lightweight forks. A fork can then be tied to a new UI session/agent that inherits previous context
Got it: **lightweight forks**, where a fork can become a new UI session/agent inheriting prior context.

column1: **What should happen to background tasks when a session is forked?**

column3: They stick around and all forks know about them until they die
Got it: **background tasks are shared across forks and remain visible until they finish or are stopped**.

column1: **Should background tasks belong to the workspace globally, or to a session/fork lineage with global visibility?**

column3: Workspacr
Got it: **background tasks are workspace-global**.

column1: **What provider model do you want in v1?**
- custom provider plugin layer over go-ai

column3: Go-ai with compatibility with pi settings files for all providers. Full settings compatibility.
Got it: **go-ai as the provider layer**, with **full compatibility with pi settings files** across providers.

column1: **Do you want gi to preserve pi’s model/provider naming and config semantics exactly, or only be able to import/read them compatibly?**

column3: Preserve
Got it: **preserve pi naming and config semantics exactly**.

column1: **What should the keychain/secrets model be in v1?**

column3: Piclaw, with the current env injection features for all tools, scripts, skills, etc. Direct port
Got it: **direct Piclaw keychain port**, including **env injection for tools, scripts, skills, etc.**

column1: **What should the artifact/attachment model be in v1?**

column3: All the above, like piclaw, including indexing. We will need to support the same viewing UX in the web and try to render images in advanced terminals like ghostty
Got it: **full Piclaw-style attachments/artifacts**, including:

column1: - image rendering in capable terminals like Ghostty

column3: All of the above.
Got it: **all of the above** for scheduling.

column1: **What should the TUI be able to do in v1 that the web UI can do too?**
I’m asking to pin down the required parity surface.

column3: we should look into what go-tui supports, but it should be minimalistic. Good scrollback, minimal formatting, expandable text field, equivalent progress and status notifications to what piclaw currrently has in its compose box
Got it: **minimalist TUI**, with:

column1: - progress/status notifications equivalent to Piclaw’s compose box
**What is the minimum CLI surface for v1?**
- send a prompt to a session

column3: All the above. It should manage the keychain, run scripts directly, take a prompt, prune state, search, etc.
Got it: **full operational CLI** in v1, including:
All the above within reason for attachments. SQLite FTS
Got it: **SQLite FTS-backed search** across:

column1: **What should “within reason for attachments” mean in v1?**

column3: File name, metadata, text indexing
Got it: **attachments in v1 should support filename, metadata, and text indexing**.

column1: **What should the web UI minimally include in v1 besides chat itself?**

column3: Identical to piclaw, with the same UI extensions (but plain JS)
Got it: **web UI v1 should be functionally identical to Piclaw**, including the same UI extensions, but implemented in **plain JS**.

column1: **Do you want gi to keep Piclaw’s message/content model exactly, or should we simplify it in v1?**

column3: What do you mean
I mean the internal structure of chat/output items.

column1: For example, Piclaw has a fairly rich message model with things like:
**Should gi preserve that same rich structured message model, or do you want a simpler internal model and just render it similarly in the UI?**

column3: Same
Got it: **same structured message/content model as Piclaw**.

column1: **Should gi v1 preserve Piclaw’s Adaptive Cards and dashboard widgets exactly, or just the underlying message/attachment primitives first?**

column3: No adaptive cards. Simpler confirmation dialogs and tables drop-downs/checkboxes and a submit button. I will want the interactive widget and inline charting
Got it:

column1: **Should those interactive UI elements exist only in the web UI in v1, or do you want TUI equivalents from the start too?**

column3: TUI only for forms and image previews in advanced terminals
Got it: **web gets the full interactive UI**, and **TUI only needs forms plus image previews in capable terminals**.

column1: **What should image preview support look like in v1?**
- inline terminal preview when supported, fallback to path/metadata otherwise

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of the above** for image previews.

column1: **What should gi store in SQLite in v1 besides chat/messages/attachments/keychain?**

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of the above** in SQLite for v1.

column1: **What should gi log and expose for observability in v1?**
- all of the above, or a filtered subset

column3: All the above. We can use SQLite JSON storage for the vast majority of it
Got it: **all of the above for observability**, with **SQLite JSON storage for most event data**.

column1: **What should the session rotation model be in v1?**
- automatic rollover when context gets too large
- same visible chat, different underlying turn/session segments

column3: Automatic rollover based on timeline length per session
Got it: **automatic session rollover based on timeline length per session**.

column1: **When a session rolls over, should the new session inherit full prior context, a compacted summary/context pack, or both?**

column3: Compacted context and the last 10 messages or so (setting variable), rebuilt from database data
Got it: **automatic rollover builds a new session from DB data using compacted context plus the last N messages**, with **N configurable**.

column1: **What should trigger context compaction in v1?**

column3: All the above except timeline length. That should not be a direct factor since context won’t cover the entire timeline thanks to summaries
Got it: **compaction triggers on token/context limits and recovery paths, not timeline length**.

column1: **Who generates the summaries/compacted context in v1?**

column3: Whatever pi does plus custom compaction hooks we will define
Got it: **start with Pi-style summarization/compaction behavior**, and allow **custom compaction hooks** later.

column1: **What should happen when compaction fails in v1?**
- fall back to a cheaper/smaller model
- abort the turn but preserve recovery state

column3: We need to have all the options here, but I don’t know how to deal with a separate model yet. We will need to make it a setting
Got it: **compaction failure handling should be policy-driven and configurable**, with support for:

column1: And the **separate compaction model should be a setting**, even if we don’t lock that design yet.
**What should happen when a provider call fails mid-turn in v1?**
- always return control immediately with status

column3: Automatic retry with exponential backoff (go-ai will help but needs setting) preserving partial output if everything fails but surfacing information to the user of retry progress and strategies
Got it: **automatic retry with exponential backoff**, **preserve partial output on ultimate failure**, and **show retry progress/strategy to the user during the turn**.

column1: **What should happen when a tool call fails mid-turn in v1?**
- mark tool result as error and let the model continue
- all of the above via policy

column3: Depends on the tool. Stateful tools need to be handled carefully, but in general retries surfaced to the model and user, so that scripts and skills can handle it
Got it: **tool failure policy is tool-specific**, especially for stateful tools, but in general:

column1: - retries should be visible to the **user**
- retries/failures should be visible to the **model**
- scripts/skills should be able to handle recovery
**How should tools declare whether they are safe to retry in v1?**

column3: Metadata. We can reuse the piclaw metadata approach for discovery, hints, etc
Got it: **tool retry/safety behavior should come from metadata**, reusing the **Piclaw-style metadata/discovery/hints approach**.

column1: **What should a tool definition minimally contain in v1?**

column3: The above, as used in piclaw. Plus hooks for progress reporting when called.
Got it: **Piclaw-style tool definitions**, plus **progress-reporting hooks during execution**.

column1: **How should progress reporting work in v1?**
- structured progress events with all of the above

column3: All the above. Piclaw model
Got it: **Piclaw-style structured progress events**, including:

column1: **What should the permissions/sandbox model be in v1, given you don’t want approval flows?**

column3: Full local trust for v1. Sandboxing is an OS concern. Some tools might have boundaries in 1.1
Got it: **full local trust in v1**, with sandboxing treated as an OS concern; tool boundaries can come later in 1.1.

column1: **What should gi’s configuration model look like in v1?**

column3: Pi/piclaw settings files plus occasional metadata and minimal critical env overrides (keychain master key, workspace details, etc)
Got it: **Pi/Piclaw-compatible settings files first**, plus **small critical env overrides** for bootstrap/runtime essentials.

column1: **What language/runtime constraints do you want for gi itself?**
- pure Go as much as possible

column3: The four you mention are critical. Tinygo for creating WASM components, bun as scripting tool for vendoring and bundling, plain JS to simplify writing code
Got it. Critical stack so far:

column1: **What should gi’s extension model be in v1?**
- load local extensions from the workspace

column3: There are both UX and tooling scopes there. Separate them
Agreed.

column1: **Next question: what should the _tooling extension model_ be in v1?**
Ignore UI/UX extensions for now. Just tools/skills/runtime behavior.

column3: Built in tools plus scripts plus MCP/ACP. Nothing else. We will be smart about our MCP adapters. QuickJS is an option for 1.1 after we define the internal scripting bridge (filesystem, internal state, sockets, web requests)
Got it: **tooling extensions in v1 = built-in tools + Joker scripts**, with **MCP/ACP in 1.1**, and possible **QuickJS in 1.1** after the scripting bridge is defined.

column1: **What should the _UI/UX extension model_ be in v1?**
Ignore tools now. Just web/TUI extensibility.

column3: Web and TUI will define core interfaces for status, drafts, intents, input, streaming, slash commands that will be shared, and web will have additional SSE handling for live widgets, codemirror, and the pane management that we have in piclaw for editors, etc.
Got it: **shared core UI interfaces** for web and TUI, with **web-specific extras** for SSE widgets, CodeMirror, and pane management.

column1: **What should slash commands cover in v1?**

column3: All the above
Got it: **slash commands should cover all of the above**.

column1: **Do you want gi v1 to support editing/opening files in the web UI like Piclaw does, or is chat + panes enough initially?**

column3: I need the workspace browser and the e editor panes, and they should be openable by the agent
Got it: **workspace browser + editor panes are required in v1**, and the **agent must be able to open them**.

column1: **What editing capabilities must those panes support in v1?**

column3: All the above, starting with the piclaw feature set
Got it: **start from Piclaw’s editor/browser feature set**, including all of the above.

column1: **What should gi’s auth/login model be in v1?**
- local user identity with web session auth

column3: Same as piclaw: no multi user, but with the option for Totp and webauthn
Got it: **same as Piclaw** for auth in v1:

column1: **What should gi’s startup/deployment model be in v1?**
- terminal-first command that can also host the web UI

column3: Single binary maintained by supervisor/systemd in web mode, cli and tui share database but runas separate processes. I do not expect a lot of contention that SQLite WAL can’t cope with
Got it: **single binary**, with:

column1: - all sharing the same **SQLite WAL** database
**What should gi’s test strategy be in v1?**

column3: All of the above plus vetting and fuzzing
Got it: **all of the above**, plus **vetting** and **fuzzing**.

column1: **What is the MVP boundary for gi v1?**
What must exist before you’d consider it usable enough to start migrating from Piclaw?

column3: what we described so far
Got it: **the MVP is exactly the surface we’ve described so far**—not a reduced subset.

column1: **What should gi be called internally and externally?**
- or a different internal naming scheme?

column3: Gi
Got it: **Gi**.

column1: **What should the binary/module naming be in v1?**
Or do you want something else?

column3: That’s good. rcarmo/gi already exists
Got it:

column1: **Do you want Gi v1 to keep Pi/Piclaw’s kanban/work item system, or leave project/task tracking out entirely?**

column3: It should be left out and decided upon as a skill. The kanban YAML format piclaw edits should be enough for other uses, but we will use GitHub and the gh tool for development
Got it: **no built-in kanban/task system**. Treat it as a **skill**, and keep the existing **kanban YAML format** available if needed. For development, use **GitHub + `gh`**.

column1: **Do you want Gi v1 to support Git/GitHub operations as built-in tools, or should that also live in scripts/skills initially?**

column3: no built in tools for that. Gh and skills
Got it: **no built-in Git/GitHub tooling** — use **`gh` + skills**.

column1: **Do you want Gi v1 to include built-in web search/browser automation, or should that also be deferred to scripts/skills?**

column3: Scripts/skills, but we will have some sort of socket/http tool for scripts to use once we define that
Got it: **web search/browser automation via scripts/skills**, with some future **socket/HTTP capability for scripts** once that bridge is defined.

column1: **What is the first real workflow Gi must handle end-to-end better than Piclaw?**

column3: Developing itself
Got it: **Gi’s first proving workflow is self-hosted development of Gi itself**.

column1: **What is the single most important thing Gi must do better than Piclaw when developing itself?**

column3: Reliability and ease of maintenance. Top spec dependencies besides go-tui and go-ai
Got it: **reliability**, **ease of maintenance**, and **minimal top-level dependencies** beyond `go-tui` and `go-ai`.

column1: **What parts of Piclaw are the biggest maintenance burden that Gi should avoid or redesign first?**

column3: Turn management and failure retries. Piclaw is very unreliable in session handling in general
Got it: **turn management, failure retries, and session handling are the first things Gi should redesign**.

column1: **What is the worst session-handling failure mode you want Gi to eliminate first?**

column3: Context compaction mid turn and the associated retries, which should be automatic
Got it: **automatic handling of mid-turn context compaction and its retries** is the first failure mode to eliminate.

column1: **When mid-turn compaction happens, should Gi hide that from the user unless it fails, or always show a visible status/progress event for it?**

column3: Always show a UI hint.
Got it: **always show a UI hint when mid-turn compaction happens**.

column1: **Should Gi treat compaction as part of the same turn, or should it create an explicit internal sub-turn/recovery step with its own logged events?**

column3: Part of the same turn
Got it: **compaction stays inside the same turn**, but with visible logged/progress events.

column1: **What should a turn look like internally in v1?**
- one request → many recoverable internal phases
- append-only event log with resumable checkpoints

column3: Simplest possible thing
Understood: **simplest possible turn model**.

column1: **If you had to choose, should that simplest turn model be based on an explicit state machine, or just an append-only event log with a small amount of turn state?**

column3: Event log.
Got it: **append-only event log with minimal turn state**.

column1: **What should be the minimum checkpoint/resume boundary inside a turn?**

column3: Tool call. Persistence is implied
Got it: **tool-call boundaries are the minimum resume checkpoints**, with persistence implied.

column1: **Besides tool calls, should Gi also checkpoint at provider retry/compaction boundaries, or only log those as events within the same resumable segment?**

column3: Checkpoint, but not fail the turn
Got it: **provider retry and compaction boundaries should also create checkpoints**, but **must not fail the turn by themselves**.

column1: **What should count as a completed turn in v1?**
- provider failed but partial output + recovery info were persisted
- tool failure was surfaced and control returned cleanly
- all of the above, if control is back with consistent state

column3: All of the above
Got it: **a turn is complete whenever control returns cleanly with consistent state**, whether it ended in success, partial success, or surfaced failure.

column1: **What should Gi show the user during a turn, in the minimum useful progress view?**
- all of the above or a smaller subset

column3: All the above using piclaw conventions. Most can translate to TUI
Got it: **full turn progress visibility using Piclaw conventions**, with most of it mapped into TUI too.

column1: **What should happen if Gi itself crashes during a turn?**
- automatically resume the turn from the last checkpoint
- mark it recoverable and ask the user
- preserve partial output and return control
- all of the above via policy

column3: Preserve and return
Got it: **on Gi crash during a turn, preserve state and return control**.

column1: **Should Gi attempt automatic turn recovery after a crash on next startup, or only show the preserved failed/recoverable state and let the user decide?**

column3: No auto recovery upon crash. Auto recovery if restarted cleanly
Got it: **no auto-recovery after a crash**, but **auto-recovery is allowed after a clean restart**.

column1: **What should count as a clean restart in v1?**
- normal process termination with state flushed

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of the above count as a clean restart**.

column1: **What should Gi do on startup if it finds unfinished turns from a clean restart?**
- mark them as resuming and replay progress in the UI
- resume none until the user opens the session

column3: Resume them automatically
Got it: **unfinished turns from a clean restart should resume automatically**.

column1: **When an unfinished turn resumes automatically, should Gi replay the prior progress/status events in the UI, or only show new events from the resume point onward?**

column3: We should see the last progress status
Got it: on auto-resume, **show the last progress status**, then continue from there.

column1: **Should Gi allow more than one turn to run at the same time across different sessions in v1, or should turn execution be globally serialized?**

column3: More than one
Got it: **multiple turns can run concurrently across sessions**.

column1: **Should Gi allow more than one turn at the same time _within the same session_, or serialize turns per session?**

column3: Serialize
Got it: **turns run concurrently across sessions, but are serialized within each session**.

column1: **If the user submits a new prompt to a session while a turn is still running, what should Gi do?**

column3: Queue, using piclaw’s queue/reorder/steer approach
Got it: **queue new prompts within a session**, using **Piclaw’s queue/reorder/steer model**.

column1: **Should queued prompts be editable/reorderable/cancellable while waiting in v1, or just reorderable like Piclaw?**

column3: Reorderable/cancellable
Got it: **queued prompts should be reorderable and cancellable** in v1.

column1: **Should queued prompts also be editable before they run, or should editing require cancel-and-resubmit?**

column3: Cancel/resubmit
Got it: **queued prompts are reorderable/cancellable, but editing is cancel-and-resubmit**.

column1: **Should Gi support draft prompts per session in v1, shared across web and TUI?**

column3: No. Local state only, history navigation with cursor keys per session
Got it: **drafts stay local to each UI**, with **per-session input history navigation** via cursor keys.

column1: **Should slash-command history and prompt history be shared within a session, or kept separate?**

column3: Separate between sessions
Got it: **history should be session-local**, not shared across sessions.

column1: **Should command history and prompt history be separate within a session, or merged into one history stream?**

column3: Separate, but tools can search the entire history for all sessions (like messages in piclaw and the scripting bridge)
Got it: **prompt history and slash-command history are separate within each session**, while tools/scripts can still search the full cross-session history.

column1: **Should Gi have a built-in notion of intents in v1, like Piclaw’s compose-box intent/status model, or should that remain just UI metadata over normal prompts?**

column3: Exactly like piclaw. Maybe some minor unification for intents and some in flight notifications like compaction
Got it: **keep Piclaw’s intent model essentially as-is**, with maybe some light unification around intents and in-flight notifications like compaction.
Those are good
Got it: v1 intents include:

column1: **Should interrupts/cancels try to stop the currently running provider/tool immediately, or just mark the turn as cancelled and let the runtime unwind safely?**

column3: Try immediately
Got it: **interrupt/cancel should try to stop the active provider/tool immediately**.

column1: **If immediate cancellation fails, should Gi keep trying in the background, or should it mark the turn as cancelling and return control while cleanup continues?**

column3: Cancelling
Got it: if immediate stop fails, Gi should mark the turn as **cancelling** and return control while cleanup continues.

column1: **What should the user be allowed to do in a session while a previous turn is still in `cancelling` state?**

column3: Queue more prompts and change settings, issue more commands - like in piclaw
Got it: while a turn is **cancelling**, the user can still:

column1: **Should a `cancelling` turn still block execution of queued prompts in that session, or can the next queued prompt start before cancellation cleanup fully finishes?**

column3: Blocks. But I expect cancellation to be definitive and effective, killing subprecesses and preserving outputs
Got it: **`cancelling` still blocks the session queue**, and cancellation must be **definitive/effective**—kill subprocesses and preserve outputs.

column1: **What should Gi do if a subprocess/tool ignores cancellation and refuses to die?**

column3: That should never happen
Got it: **design assumption for v1 is that cancellation is enforceable**—subprocesses/tools must die when cancelled.

column1: **What should Gi do with partial tool/provider output when a turn is cancelled?**
- discard tool logs but keep model output

column3: Preserve and show with a clip annotation oof provenance
Got it: **preserve and show partial output**, with a clear **clipped/cancelled provenance annotation**.

column1: **What should Gi use as the canonical artifact storage layout in v1?**

column3: Workspace paths and db managed blobs for images and generated outputs the user explicitly asks for, attached by the internal tool. Referenced files should be rendered in the web as clickable pills to open the editor/workspace, and there will be fts metadata for some items like piclaw. When you write the implementation checklist that should prompt you to investigate further
Got it:

column1: - **filesystem/workspace paths** as the main artifact layout
- **DB-managed blobs** for images and explicit generated outputs attached by the internal tool
- referenced files rendered in web UI as **clickable pills** opening editor/workspace
- some artifact metadata indexed in **FTS**, Piclaw-style
- implementation checklist should explicitly flag this area for deeper investigation
**What should Gi’s internal “messages” capability cover in v1?**

column3: All of the above
Got it: **all of the above** for `messages` in v1.

column1: **Should `messages` be a built-in internal tool only, or also exposed as a CLI/TUI/web user-facing capability directly?**

column3: both
Got it: **`messages` should be both an internal tool and a direct user-facing capability**.

column1: **What should the `exit` capability do in v1?**
- request clean shutdown/restart of the Gi web service
- all of the above, depending on context

column3: Stop and exit the current process cleanly
Got it: **`exit` should stop and exit the current process cleanly**.

column1: **What should the `schedule` capability be allowed to run in v1?**

column3: All of the above but slash commands. Tied to an explicit session
Got it: `schedule` can run:

column1: **Should scheduled jobs run under the target session’s identity/context, or as a separate scheduler identity that posts back into the session?**

column3: Separate temporary session that can post back
Got it: **scheduled jobs run in a separate temporary session** that can **post back into the target session**.

column1: **What should happen if a scheduled job fails repeatedly in v1?**
- disable the schedule after N failures
- keep running forever but post failures

column3: 1 and 2
Got it: **retry with backoff**, then **disable after N failures**.

column1: **Should schedules be visible and manageable from all three surfaces in v1 — web, TUI, and CLI?**

column3: Yes, via commands
Got it: **schedules should be visible/manageable from web, TUI, and CLI via commands**.

column1: **What should the `shell` tool guarantee in v1?**

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of the above** for `shell`.

column1: **What should the `read`/`write`/`edit` file tools guarantee in v1?**

column3: All the above
Got it: **all of the above** for `read` / `write` / `edit`.

column1: **Should `edit` in v1 be strictly exact-match replacement like Piclaw’s current tool, or do you want fallback modes too?**

column3: All of the above
Got it: **`edit` should support exact-match replacement, patch/diff mode, and model-assisted retry mode**.

column1: **What should determine which edit mode Gi uses first in v1?**

column3: Model choice
Got it: **edit strategy should be chosen based on the model**.

column1: **What should the default model-based edit policy be in v1?**
- strong models can use patch/model-assisted fallback
- weaker models are restricted to exact-match

column3: Patch
Got it: default edit policy should be **patch-oriented**.

column1: **Should Gi still try exact-match edits first when a patch-capable model is in use, or go straight to patch mode by default?**

column3: The model decides
Got it: **the model decides the edit mode**.

column1: **What metadata should a model carry in v1 besides Pi-compatible provider/model settings?**

column3: What pi has
Got it: **model metadata should match what Pi already has**.

column1: **Should Gi v1 support per-session model switching, or should model choice be fixed when the session is created?**

column3: Per session
Got it: **model choice is switchable per session**.

column1: **Should a model switch take effect immediately for the next queued turn only, or also try to affect the currently running turn when possible?**

column3: Next turn
Got it: **model switches apply on the next turn**, not the current one.

column1: **Should sessions also support per-session provider settings overrides in v1, or only model selection?**

column3: Only model selection some/commands for some parameters
Got it: **per-session model selection**, plus **some parameter changes via commands**, but not arbitrary per-session provider config overrides.

column1: **Which per-session parameters must be command-adjustable in v1?**

column3: Thinking level and verbose
Got it: **per-session command-adjustable parameters are:**

column1: **Should `verbose` affect only UI/log detail, or also influence the model prompt/agent behavior?**

column3: Only prompt agent behavior
Got it: **`verbose` should affect prompt/agent behavior only**, not just UI logging.

column1: **Should “thinking level” map exactly to Pi’s current thinking model/semantics, or can Gi define a simpler compatible version in v1?**

column3: It has to map exactly to what model supports
Got it: **thinking level must map exactly to what the selected model supports**.

column1: **What should happen if the user asks for a thinking level the current model does not support?**
- clamp to the nearest supported level
- keep requested value pending until model changes

column3: Reject
Got it: **reject unsupported thinking levels with a clear message**.

column1: **What should the default session model be in v1?**

column3: Inherit
Got it: **inherit Pi/Piclaw default model selection logic**.

column1: **Should Gi v1 preserve Pi/Piclaw’s prompt template system too, or start with a smaller built-in system and only keep settings compatibility where necessary?**

column3: Preserve
Got it: **preserve Pi/Piclaw’s prompt template system**.

column1: **Should Gi v1 preserve Pi/Piclaw’s hook system too, or simplify hooks initially?**

column3: What do you mean?
I mean the runtime hooks around a turn and provider/tool execution.

column1: For example, Piclaw/go-ai style hooks can include things like:
**Should Gi preserve that full hook surface, or start with a smaller hook system in v1?**

column3: Smart smaller, but add those to the roadmap
Got it: **smaller, smarter hook surface in v1**, with the broader hook system on the roadmap.

column1: **Which hook points are absolutely required in v1?**

column3: All the above plus error handling
Got it: **v1 needs hooks for:**

column1: **Should v1 hooks be implemented as Joker scripts, Go interfaces, or both?**

column3: Both plus roadmap to JavaScript with quickjs
Got it: **v1 hooks should support both Go and Joker**, with **QuickJS on the roadmap**.

column1: **Which one should be the primary extension surface in v1: Joker hooks first, or Go hooks first?**

column3: Joker
Got it: **Joker is the primary hook/extension surface in v1**, with Go hooks also supported.

column1: **How should Joker hooks be loaded in v1?**

column3: All the above plus database. One of the goals is that you should be able to take all your environment with you by just copying the databaseand the binary.
Got it: **Joker hooks can come from workspace config, skills, packaged installs, and the database**, and a major goal is **portable state via just the binary + database**.

column1: **What should still live on disk outside the database in v1?**
- or should most of that also be mirrored/importable into the DB

column3: Let’s break that down one by one
Good.

column1: **Should settings/config live only on disk in v1, or also be importable/mirrored into the database?**

column3: Mirror
Got it: **settings/config should be mirrored into the database**.

column1: **Should skills live only on disk in v1, or also be importable/mirrored into the database?**

column3: Mirror and the database should be query before the file system
Got it: **skills should be mirrored into the database**, and **the database should be queried before the filesystem**.

column1: **Should scripts also be mirrored into the database, or stay filesystem-first in v1?**

column3: Also
Got it: **scripts should also be mirrored into the database**.

column1: **Should prompt templates also be mirrored into the database, or remain settings-file only in v1?**

column3: Also. There should be an asset stable with all of that kind of thing embedded and full text search
Got it: **prompt templates should also be mirrored into the database**, and there should be an **assets table** for this kind of embedded content with **full-text search**.

column1: **Should generated artifacts/files also be mirrored into the database, or only selected ones like images and explicit user-requested outputs?**

column3: Only selected ones
Got it: **only selected generated artifacts should be mirrored into the database**.

column1: **Should logs/observability data live only in SQLite in v1, or also be written to filesystem log files?**

column3: Only counters
Got it: **don’t treat filesystem logs as a first-class output**.

column1: **Do you want full observability events stored only in SQLite, with only lightweight counters/metrics elsewhere, and no normal log files?**

column3: I want counters inside the database and events, but not all of the data that is associated with log files.
Got it: **store counters and structured events in SQLite**, but **not full traditional logfile payloads**.

column1: **Should cached assets/dependencies in v1 live only on disk, or also be mirrored into the database?**

column3: What cash assets dependencies
I mean things like:

column1: - vendored JS/CSS for the web UI
**For those kinds of cached/generated support assets, should Gi keep them only on disk in v1, or also mirror some of them into the database?**

column3: Web should be packed into the binary, skills and packages should be imported, cashes, live elsewhere, thumbnails are dynamically generated and kept in a separate table for cleaning up. We will need a good image processing library.
Got it:

column1: - **thumbnails stored in a separate cleanupable table**
- and we’ll need a **good image processing library**
**Should keychain secrets themselves live in the SQLite database in v1, or should Gi keep Piclaw’s current split model if there is one?**

column3: They should live in the database encrypted in the same way
Got it: **keychain secrets should live in the database**, encrypted the same way as in Piclaw.

column1: **Should the encryption key for those secrets come only from an env var/bootstrap secret, or do you also want interactive unlock methods in v1?**

column3: Both
Got it: **both** for v1:

column1: **When Gi starts and the keychain is locked, what should happen by default?**
- CLI fails with a clear message unless explicitly interactive
- start in degraded mode until unlocked

column3: Degraded
Got it: **start in degraded mode until unlocked**.

column1: **In degraded mode, what should still work in v1?**

column3: Everything
Got it: **everything should still work in degraded mode except operations that actually need secret material**.

column1: **What should happen when a turn or tool needs a locked secret in degraded mode?**
- fail immediately with a clear unlock-needed error
- emit a UI prompt to unlock and leave the turn paused

column3: Admit the UI prompt
Got it: **emit a UI prompt to unlock**, and leave the operation waiting on that.

column1: **Should unlocking the keychain resume the blocked turn/tool automatically, or should it return control and ask the user to retry?**

column3: Resume
Got it: **unlocking should automatically resume the blocked turn/tool**.

column1: **Should the unlocked keychain stay unlocked for the whole process lifetime in v1, or should it re-lock after inactivity / on demand?**

column3: Stay
Got it: **once unlocked, it stays unlocked for the process lifetime**.

column1: **Should each process unlock independently in v1, or should unlocking in the web service also make secrets available to CLI/TUI processes through some shared mechanism?**

column3: Each process
Got it: **each process unlocks independently**.

column1: **What should the first spec artifact be for Gi?**

column3: An architecture decision record set with full checklist for implementation. That should be in a docs tree. And I want you to build all of the work items that you can using the same checklist.
Got it: **first spec artifact = a `docs/` tree with ADRs plus a full implementation checklist**, and that checklist should also drive the work items we can execute.

column1: **Do you want one top-level ADR for the whole architecture plus smaller ADRs per subsystem, or only small focused ADRs from the start?**

column3: Top + smaller. You can dump the entire contents of this chat there for reference
Got it: **one top-level ADR plus smaller subsystem ADRs**, and we can include the full chat contents there as reference.

column1: **Do you want the implementation checklist organized by subsystem, by milestone/phase, or both?**

column3: Checklists by sub system and groups of them per phase
Got it: **checklists by subsystem**, grouped into **phases**.

column1: **What should Phase 1 focus on first: turn engine, database/state model, or the web UI shell?**

column3: All the above with minimal functionality
Got it: **Phase 1 should cover turn engine, database/state model, and web UI shell together, with minimal functionality**.

column1: **What is the absolute minimum “vertical slice” that proves Gi is alive in Phase 1?**
- cleanly return control after the turn

column3: That works for me
Got it.

column1: **What should be the next vertical slice after that?**

column3: Keychain and context and skills
Got it: next slice is **keychain + context + skills**.

column1: **Of those three, which one must be implemented first: keychain, context compaction/recovery, or skills?**

column3: Context. Ask me only things that you cannot figure out from what we’ve discussed so far and the design of piclaw
